top_categories.workout_context: When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
68 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 68 miles.
Base fitness for Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca. Train it until it feels boring.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 68 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.
At Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca’s 68 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Raul Alcala Challenge Oaxaca demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 68 miles of mixed terrain in Oaxaca de Juárez, Oaxaca, Mexico.
